pattern: Counting distinct palindromic subsequences over a tiny alphabet uses an interval DP that, on matching ends, jumps to the next inner occurrence of that character to avoid double counting.
hints: Distinctness is the hard part; two equal subsequences must be counted once.
Define dp over substrings; when the endpoints differ, use inclusion-exclusion of the inner intervals.

When endpoints match on character c, find the next c from the left and the previous c from the right to handle the three cases (no inner c, one inner c, two or more) without overcounting; take everything modulo 1e9+7.

stress.description: random short string over the four-letter alphabet (kept small for the brute-force oracle)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(14, size)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
